payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Turn the reviewer-infrastructure-unavailable BLOCK into a POLICY-GATED RECOVERY so the workflow can make safe forward progress past a reviewer that failed to return a verdict, without faking a verdict or bypassing the gate. Add reviewer_unavailable_policy in {block, escalate, proceed_degraded} default escalate, wire config/API/CLI, make block preserve current behavior, make escalate route to resumable human escalation, make proceed_degraded advance only on Claude+Codex accept while recording degraded evidence and never counting missing Cursor as accept, keep real Cursor revise/deny blocking, force high-stakes/agentic/runtime-native paths to escalate, preserve traceability/replay, and keep lead_direct default unchanged.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: PRD addresses every named intent requirement: policy enum {block,escalate,proceed_degraded} default escalate, config/API/CLI wiring, block preserves PR#2, escalate=resumable, proceed_degraded gated on Claude+Codex accept with degraded receipt, missing Cursor never accept, real Cursor revise/deny blocks, high-stakes forced escalate, lead_direct default unchanged
All three high-severity PRD grill findings resolved; G4 waiver matches out-of-scope intent
Implementation files in worktree match implementation-plan file list, de-risking feasibility

Replay hash integrity verified: not met (approval required)
evidence: Gate should ADVANCE: PRD is well-formed, grounded, and resolves high-severity risks

confidence.rationale: PRD is internally consistent, grounded in verified source anchors, and resolves all high-severity grill findings; intent requirements are each mapped to a promise with allowed/forbidden outcomes. Confidence held below 0.95 by one promise/test coverage gap (P6 user-facing) and two unverified items (replay hashes, resume machinery) that I could not close in-gate.

payload.content: PRD for turning reviewer-infrastructure-unavailable BLOCK into a policy-gated recovery is sound, grounded in real source anchors (PR #2 classification, runtime_native/agentic predicates, lead_direct default), and resolves all high-severity grill findings. Accept with one revision note: P6/G3 promise user-facing high-stakes escalation but the TDD only tests agentic-required and runtime-native.

The user-facing category in the forbidden-outcome is unverified, so a degraded auto-proceed on a user-facing high-stakes path could slip through untested.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Turn the reviewer-infrastructure-unavailable BLOCK into a POLICY-GATED RECOVERY so the workflow can make safe forward progress past a reviewer that failed to return a verdict, without faking a verdict or bypassing the gate. Add reviewer_unavailable_policy in {block, escalate, proceed_degraded} default escalate, wire config/API/CLI, make block preserve current behavior, make escalate route to resumable human escalation, make proceed_degraded advance only on Claude+Codex accept while recording degraded evidence and never counting missing Cursor as accept, keep real Cursor revise/deny blocking, force high-stakes/agentic/runtime-native paths to escalate, preserve traceability/replay, and keep lead_direct default unchanged.

claims: PRD premise that recoverable Cursor infrastructure failures currently terminate the gate is consistent with the workflow block branch
All 6 PRD promises have at least one mapped RED/GREEN test
Default policy escalate (not proceed_degraded) preserves conservative behavior

criteria: No untestable/contradictory promise: one minor gap (P6)
evidence: P6 wording should be narrowed to {agentic-required, runtime-native} OR a high-stakes predicate definition+test added at the TDD gate
confidence.rationale: PRD is concrete, internally consistent, and its load-bearing premise plus dependent infrastructure are verified directly in source. Confidence is held below 0.9 by one unresolved definitional gap (P6 high-stakes predicate) and because this is a planning gate with no tests executed yet.

payload.content: PRD for reviewer-unavailable recovery is internally consistent and its premise is verified against current source (PR #2 classification, resume-signal machinery, runtime_native evidence grade all exist). 6 promises map cleanly to 4 issues and 7 RED/GREEN tests; all grill findings resolved/waived; default escalate keeps a safe default and forbidden outcomes bar counting missing Cursor as accept. Accept with one minor objection: P6/story-5 promise protection for a 'user-facing/high-stakes' path that has no concrete predicate or test beyond agentic-required and runtime-native.

assumptions_to_verify: At GREEN, force_next_round/review-packet logic must accept policy-authorized degraded evidence or P2/P3 will still block
'Codex accepts' in P2 means Codex deterministic checks pass; exact acceptance predicate to be pinned at implementation
contradictions_checked: PRD claims PR #2 classification exists -> confirmed in cursor_agent.py and codex_supervisor_stdio.py:3345
PRD claims resumable Continue escalation machinery exists -> confirmed at codex_supervisor_stdio.py:2577/2584/3407
PRD default escalate vs auto-proceed -> consistent across PRD, issues, grill G2
PRD claims missing Cursor never counted as accept -> consistent with grill G1 and TDD TG2 assertions
missing_evidence: A concrete predicate defining 'user-facing/high-stakes' evidence separate from agentic_lead_policy=required and required_evidence_grade=runtime_native

Confirmation that the review-packet force_next_round path (codex_supervisor_stdio.py:1191) will recognize policy-authorized degraded evidence rather than re-block (noted as a risk in the implementation plan, not yet a test)
critical_review.strongest_objection: P6 and user story 5 promise that 'user-facing/high-stakes' evidence paths escalate instead of auto-proceeding, but neither the issues nor the TDD define a predicate or test for that category distinct from agentic-required and runtime-native — the promise is broader than its planned coverage.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the workflow block branch cannot actually be reached by recoverable infrastructure failures (premise false), or that the resume-signal machinery cannot carry authorized degraded evidence forward, would move me to deny. A defined predicate or added test for the high-stakes category would move me to unqualified accept.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Turn the reviewer-infrastructure-unavailable BLOCK into a POLICY-GATED RECOVERY so the workflow can make safe forward progress past a reviewer that failed to return a verdict, without faking a verdict or bypassing the gate. Add reviewer_unavailable_policy in {block, escalate, proceed_degraded} default escalate, wire config/API/CLI, make block preserve current behavior, make escalate route to resumable human escalation, make proceed_degraded advance only on Claude+Codex accept while recording degraded evidence and never counting missing Cursor as accept, keep real Cursor revise/deny blocking, force high-stakes/agentic/runtime-native paths to escalate, preserve traceability/replay, and keep lead_direct default unchanged.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: All 6 PRD promises are referenced by at least one issue
Every PRD promise has at least one mapped TDD test case
All PRD and TDD grill findings are resolved or appropriately waived
ISS-4 acceptance criteria omit the user-facing/high-stakes escalation path named in P6/G3

criteria: All PRD promises covered by issues
confidence.rationale: Promise-level mapping is complete, traceable, and well-scoped with concrete acceptance criteria and a matching TDD plan; deduction reflects two unverified coverage soft spots (user-facing/high-stakes escalation and P2 negative case) plus SHA integrity check not executed.

payload.content: Issues ISS-1..ISS-4 map completely to PRD promises P1-P6; acceptance criteria are concrete and testable; PRD and TDD grill findings all resolved/waived. Accept with two tdd-level objections: P6 'user-facing/high-stakes' escalation path and P2 negative (Claude/Codex non-accept) case lack explicit acceptance criteria/tests.

assumptions_to_verify: 'user-facing/high-stakes' in P6 is umbrella language subsumed by the two concrete enforced signals rather than a distinct enforced path

Planning artifacts on disk match the handoff SHA256 hashes

Whether real Cursor revise/deny is reclassified as unavailable — refuted: ISS-4 + P5 keep AND-verdict blocking path
missing_evidence: SHA256 verification of the five planning artifacts against handoff hashes was not executed (command required approval)
implementation-plan.md content not inspected
No explicit issue/test asserting proceed_degraded does NOT advance when Claude or Codex rejects
critical_review.strongest_objection: P6 and grill finding G3 promise that proceed_degraded cannot auto-fire for user-facing/high-stakes evidence, but ISS-4 acceptance criteria and the TDD test cases only enumerate agentic-required and runtime-native signals, leaving the user-facing/high-stakes path without an explicit criterion or test and at risk of silent omission in implementation.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that 'user-facing/high-stakes' is a distinct enforced evidence path in the codebase with no covering acceptance criterion or test would change the decision from accept to revise; an artifact SHA mismatch would change it to deny.

No full bypass (advance still requires Claude+Codex accept) and untested. Verdict: revise.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Corrective outcome-review retry. The previous outcome_review blocked only because the lead missed the supervisor-owned pytest evidence. Codex has now attached durable test evidence at docs/dual-agent/reviewer-unavailable-recovery-20260531/test-evidence.md and matching tool receipts: pytest-full-20260531 = uv run --extra dev pytest -q -> 526 passed in 65.77s, and pytest-focused-reviewer-unavailable-20260531 -> 8 passed in 11.92s. Verify the file if needed. Review the implementation normally; do not count missing Cursor verdicts as accept. If Cursor is still reviewer_contract_unmet/reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable and Claude+Codex accept, recover through the policy-gated reviewer-unavailable path.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

uv run --extra dev pytest -q <8 reviewer-unavailable cases> -> 8 passed in 11.92s (pytest-focused-reviewer-unavailable-20260531)
confidence.rationale: Evidence is verified and most PRD promises are test-backed, so confidence in the assessment is high; the single blocking issue is a confirmed, reproducible logic gap traced through both the plan and handler, not speculation. Confidence is not higher because sha256 of artifacts was not re-hashed (approval required) and the block+safety path was confirmed by static trace rather than a live failing test.

payload.content: Test evidence verified (526 full / 8 focused passed) and PRD promises P1,P2,P3,P5,P6 hold with test coverage. One IMPORTANT P4 fidelity gap: reviewer_unavailable_policy=block combined with a safety reason (agentic-required/runtime_native/user-facing) escalates instead of blocking, records a contradictory decision=block in the ledger, and creates a non-resumable dead-end loop. Does not affect the current task config (agentic off, escalate default) but violates a stated forbidden outcome. Recommend revise.

assumptions_to_verify: block + agentic_lead_policy=required is an operator-reachable configuration (no upstream guard observed forbidding it)
On human Continue, the plan re-evaluates and re-hits decision=block, re-escalating indefinitely
contradictions_checked: Whether the block-only test (line 1423) already covers block+safety -> it does not; it uses default agentic policy so forced_by_safety is False
Whether proceed_degraded can fire without available reviewers accepting -> it cannot; gated by available_reviewers_accept in both plan and forced codex accept
Whether a genuine Cursor revise/deny could be recovered as infra-unavailable -> it cannot; recoverable classification requires an infra failure signal
missing_evidence: No test exercises reviewer_unavailable_policy=block together with agentic_lead_policy=required / runtime_native / user_facing
sha256 of planning artifacts and test-evidence.md not re-verified this round (command required approval; content read and consistent instead)
critical_review.strongest_objection: reviewer_unavailable_policy=block combined with a safety reason violates PRD P4's forbidden outcome (block mode escalates) and produces a contradictory ledger entry plus a non-resumable dead-end loop, because the plan's canonical_policy==block branch precedes the authorization branch while the handler's block branch is guarded by 'not forced_by_safety'.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Either an upstream guard that prevents block policy from coexisting with safety reasons, or a passing test showing block+agentic-required returns a clean, non-escalating blocked result consistent with P4 — either would downgrade the objection and allow accept.
